description.en: These are string constants containing Bluetooth addresses with special meanings. For example, BDADDR_ANY can be used to indicate any address when specifying the binding socket with BTPROTO_RFCOMM.

excerpt.es: These are string constants containing Bluetooth addresses with special meanings. For example, BDADDR_ANY can be used to indicate any address when specifying the binding socket with BTPROTO_RFCOMM.
excerpt.en: These are string constants containing Bluetooth addresses with special meanings. For example, BDADDR_ANY can be used to indicate any address when specifying the binding socket with BTPROTO_RFCOMM.

description.en: These constants describe the Bluetooth address type when binding or connecting a BTPROTO_L2CAP socket. Availability: Linux, FreeBSD Added in version 3.14.

excerpt.es: These constants describe the Bluetooth address type when binding or connecting a BTPROTO_L2CAP socket. Availability: Linux, FreeBSD Added in version 3.14.
excerpt.en: These constants describe the Bluetooth address type when binding or connecting a BTPROTO_L2CAP socket. Availability: Linux, FreeBSD Added in version 3.14.

description.en: Bind the socket to address. The socket must not already be bound. (The format of address depends on the address family — see above.) Raises an auditing event socket.bind with arguments self, address. Availability: not WASI.
